heading: Actions involving discrimination

children.label: A
children.text: has been affected by an action which the employee or applicant may appeal to the Merit Systems Protection Board, and

children.label: i
children.text: section 717 of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (),42 U.S.C. 2000e–16

children.label: ii
children.text: section 6(d) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (),29 U.S.C. 206(d)

children.label: iii
children.text: section 501 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (),29 U.S.C. 791

children.label: iv
children.text: sections 12 and 15 of the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 (, 633a), or29 U.S.C. 631

children.label: v
children.text: any rule, regulation, or policy directive prescribed under any provision of law described in clauses (i) through (iv) of this subparagraph,

children.label: A
children.text: any action described in paragraph (1)(A) of this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: any issue of discrimination prohibited under any provision of law described in paragraph (1)(B) of this subsection;

children.label: A
children.text: the date of issuance of the decision if the employee or applicant does not file a petition with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission under subsection (b)(1) of this section, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date the Commission determines not to consider the decision under subsection (b)(2) of this section.
An employee or applicant may, within 30 days after notice of the decision of the Board under subsection (a)(1) of this section, petition the Commission to consider the decision.

The Commission shall, within 30 days after the date of the petition, determine whether to consider the decision. A determination of the Commission not to consider the decision may not be used as evidence with respect to any issue of discrimination in any judicial proceeding concerning that issue.

children.label: A
children.text: concur in the decision of the Board; or

children.label: i
children.text: the decision of the Board constitutes an incorrect interpretation of any provision of any law, rule, regulation, or policy directive referred to in subsection (a)(1)(B) of this section, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the decision involving such provision is not supported by the evidence in the record as a whole.

In considering any decision of the Board under this subsection, the Commission may refer the case to the Board, or provide on its own, for the taking (within such period as permits the Commission to make a decision within the 60-day period prescribed under this subsection) of additional evidence to the extent it considers necessary to supplement the record.

children.label: A
children.text: If the Commission concurs pursuant to paragraph (3)(A) of this subsection in the decision of the Board, the decision of the Board shall be a judicially reviewable action.

children.label: B
children.text: If the Commission issues any decision under paragraph (3)(B) of this subsection, the Commission shall immediately refer the matter to the Board.
concur and adopt in whole the decision of the Commission; or

children.label: i
children.text: reaffirm the initial decision of the Board; or

children.label: ii
children.text: reaffirm the initial decision of the Board with such revisions as it determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: the factual record compiled under this section,

children.label: B
children.text: the decisions issued by the Board and the Commission under this section, and

children.label: C
children.text: any transcript of oral arguments made, or legal briefs filed, before the Board or the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: The special panel shall, within 45 days after a matter has been certified to it, review the administrative record transmitted to it and, on the basis of the record, decide the issues in dispute and issue a final decision which shall be a judicially reviewable action.

children.label: B
children.text: The special panel shall give due deference to the respective expertise of the Board and Commission in making its decision.
The special panel shall refer its decision under paragraph (2) of this subsection to the Board and the Board shall order any agency to take any action appropriate to carry out the decision.

The special panel shall permit the employee or applicant who brought the complaint and the employing agency to appear before the panel to present oral arguments and to present written arguments with respect to the matter.

Upon application by the employee or applicant, the Commission may issue such interim relief as it determines appropriate to mitigate any exceptional hardship the employee or applicant might otherwise incur as a result of the certification of any matter under this subsection, except that the Commission may not stay, or order any agency to review on an interim basis, the action referred to in subsection (a)(1) of this section.

children.label: i
children.text: an individual appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, to serve for a term of 6 years as chairman of the special panel each time it is convened;

children.label: ii
children.text: one member of the Board designated by the Chairman of the Board each time a panel is convened; and

children.label: iii
children.text: one member of the Commission designated by the Chairman of the Commission each time a panel is convened.

children.label: B
children.text: The chairman is entitled to pay at a rate equal to the maximum annual rate of basic pay payable under the General Schedule for each day he is engaged in the performance of official business on the work of the special panel.

children.label: C
children.text: The Board and the Commission shall provide such administrative assistance to the special panel as may be necessary and, to the extent practicable, shall equally divide the costs of providing the administrative assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: the 120th day following the filing of any matter described in subsection (a)(2) of this section with an agency, there is no judicially reviewable action under this section or an appeal under paragraph (2) of this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: the 120th day following the filing of an appeal with the Board under subsection (a)(1) of this section, there is no judicially reviewable action (unless such action is not as the result of the filing of a petition by the employee under subsection (b)(1) of this section); or

children.label: C
children.text: the 180th day following the filing of a petition with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission under subsection (b)(1) of this section, there is no final agency action under subsection (b), (c), or (d) of this section;

If, at any time after the 120th day following the filing of any matter described in subsection (a)(2) of this section with an agency, there is no judicially reviewable action, the employee may appeal the matter to the Board under subsection (a)(1) of this section.

Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect the right to trial de novo under any provision of law described in subsection (a)(1) of this section after a judicially reviewable action, including the decision of an agency under subsection (a)(2) of this section.

children.label: f
children.text: In any case in which an employee is required to file any action, appeal, or petition under this section and the employee timely files the action, appeal, or petition with an agency other than the agency with which the action, appeal, or petition is to be filed, the employee shall be treated as having timely filed the action, appeal, or petition as of the date it is filed with the proper agency.
